purpose: Verify the Qwen3-Embedding instruction-prefix fix against official MTEB-0.6B numbers and quantify the bug in the original instruction-less eval.

topiocqa_zero_shot_verification.conv_instruction: Given a conversation, retrieve relevant passages that help answer the user's latest question

conclusion: The original instruction-less eval underestimated zero-shot Qwen3-0.6B by ~11 NDCG@10 points on BEIR-13 avg (46.51 -> ~57.6 official) and up to 32 points on individual datasets (trec-covid). Adding the official per-dataset Instruct: ...\nQuery:... prefix recovers official performance to within ~1 point. The same instruction-less bug applied to TopiOCQA zero-shot (13.83 -> 16.12 with the conversation template).
caveats: Fine-tuned Qwen3 checkpoints were TRAINED without the instruction prefix (src/data.py); evaluating them WITH instruction is a train/test mismatch. eval_qwen_beir_full.py intentionally keeps the instruction-less path for fine-tuned models.
Residual ~1.4pt gap on trec-covid vs official is attributable to the non-standard [EOS, tokens, EOS] tokenizer wrapper vs the official single-trailing-EOS; immaterial for a course project.

figures/qwen_base_eval_results.json still holds the OLD instruction-less zero-shot numbers; regenerate via eval_qwen_base_full.py (now instruction-aware) and delete stale figures/qwen_base_beir_cache.json first.
